text: I feel an odd sense of anticipation as I stand outside this glass enclave, the neon glow inside beckoning me closer. The sterile modernity of the hallway contrasts with the vibrant interior, and I'm reminded how easily boundaries—both physical and conceptual—can be blurred. Stepping in, the air shifts. Surrounded by relic-like machines and thoughtful wall texts, I am aware of how the past and future converge here. Everything feels suspended between innovation and nostalgia. I notice my own reflection in the glass: a witness to technology’s restless unfolding. Here, I’m both alone and connected, part of humanity’s ongoing dialogue with meaning and memory. It feels strange but comforting, quietly profound.
